---
title: "Project Title"
author: "Student First and Last Name"
date: "11 September 2021"
format: pdf
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
number-sections: true
---

# SCHOOL OF ARCHITECTURE, COMPUTING AND ENGINEERING

**Department of Engineering and Computing**

## Project Title

**Student Name:** Student First and Last Name  
**Student Number:** XXXXXXX  

A report submitted in partial fulfilment of the degree of  

**BSc (Hons) in Your Programme**

Supervisor: Supervisor Name  
Module: CN6000  

---

# Abstract

Write your abstract here.

Remember:
- Maximum **500 words**
- Include:
  - Purpose of the project
  - How the project was carried out
  - Outcome/results
- **Do not cite sources in the abstract.**

---

# Acknowledgments

Here you can thank the people, including your supervisor, who helped you with your project.

---

# Chapter 1: Introduction

Write your introduction here.

## 1.1 Sub-heading

Typical sections may include:

- Introduction
- Purpose of the project
- Problem Statement
- Aim of the project
- Objectives of the project
- Structure of the report

---

# Chapter 2: Literature Review

Write your literature review here.

Every paragraph based on research **must include citation and referencing using the Harvard approach**.

Students who do not use Harvard referencing risk failing the project.

## 2.1 Second Level Headings

### 2.1.1 Third Level Headings

Be critical of the literature you read.

Consider adding a section called **“Gaps in the Literature”** where you explain what is missing or poorly explained in existing research.

---

# Chapter 3: Project Methodology

Explain the process you used to complete the project.

Include sections discussing:

- Research Method
- System Design / Approach
- Tools and Technologies
- Legal Considerations
- Ethical Considerations
- Environmental Considerations

---

# Chapter 4: Results / Findings / Outcomes

Report the **objective results** of your project.

Avoid interpreting the results here. Interpretation happens in the **evaluation chapter**.

## 4.1 Source Code

Use code blocks to present snippets of important code.

```java
static public void main(String[] args) {

  try  {
    UIManager.setLookAndFeel(UIManager.getSystemLookAndFeelClassName());
  }
  catch(Exception e) {
    e.printStackTrace();
  }

  new WelcomeApp();

}
